page: UpCloud HICPU-8xCPU-12GB is a compute-optimized virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 shared vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 12.0 GB of system memory, providing 1.5 GB of RAM per core, and includes a bundled 100 GB local SSD. Network connectivity is supported by one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks place this instance consistently in the average tier for multi-core operations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test scored 6278.51, and the Geekbench multi-core test reached 6669.0. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with stress-ng single-core performance scoring 788.68, placing it in the bottom 10% of tested servers. Memory bandwidth remains stable, measuring 41.28 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 29.12 GB/sec for large block write operations.

This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for multi-threaded workloads that require high parallel compute capacity but have modest memory footprints. It is well-suited for tasks such as software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded decompression. It is less suitable for memory-intensive databases or single-threaded applications that depend heavily on high single-core clock speeds.
description: UpCloud HICPU-8xCPU-12GB is a compute-optimized virtual server utilizing the KVM hypervisor. It is configured with 8 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor clocked at 2.0 GHz, alongside 12.0 GB of RAM and a 100 GB local SSD. While single-core performance is limited, scoring in the bottom 10% on stress-ng benchmarks, its multi-core capabilities are competitive, achieving average tier results in Geekbench and PassMark tests. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for parallelized workloads with low memory requirements, such as web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing.

tagline: A compute-focused virtual server offering eight shared AMD EPYC cores and local SSD storage for parallelized, low-memory workloads.

12.0 GB of system memory and 100 GB local SSD storage
